commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b0783ee4ee2e0e0efeba9da8db4e480c74c55de4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional assistant-response acknowledgement after queued transcript commit for active requester completion wakes and updates focused subagent delivery tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +42. Total +90 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source and the PR helper test model the key interleaving, and dispatch source shows a direct-failure completion can still enter fallback steering where transcript commit remains the only delivery proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The existing PR branch needs a contributor or maintainer update for the incomplete delivery path and the mock-only proof gate; automation should not take over while contributor real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: fix(subagents): require assistant ack for completion wake
target.body:
Fixes #92433

## Summary

- Require completion handoffs queued into an active requester to be followed by an assistant response before marking the wake as delivered.
- Treat a requester `agent_end` after the queued completion user message, but before assistant consumption, as an unconfirmed wake so the existing requester-agent fallback can deliver the completion instead of silently dropping it.
- Keep ordinary non-completion steering on the existing transcript-commit boundary.

## Fix classification

Root-cause reliability fix for subagent completion delivery acknowledgement.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

High for the source-level race covered here. The fix narrows the delivered boundary from transcript commit to requester consumption only for completion handoff paths, and related local runtime tests pass.

## Root cause

The active requester wake path used `waitForTranscriptCommit` as the delivery proof for subagent completion handoffs. That only proves the internal completion message was committed as a queued user entry. If the requester run then emitted `agent_end` before producing any assistant response, the completion was still reported as delivered and the direct completion fallback did not run.

## Why this is root-cause fix

The requester transcript event stream is the source of truth for whether the queued completion was merely committed or actually consumed. The patch adds a completion-only option that waits for an assistant `message_start`/`message_end` after the matching queued user `message_end`; if `agent_end` arrives first, the wake rejects as unconsumed and returns a non-queued outcome to the existing fallback path.

## Patch quality notes

The diff is limited to the embedded queue wait helper, the subagent completion active-requester wake options, and regression expectations. It does not add sleeps, widen channel transport behavior, or change the completion direct-first dispatch ordering.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Subagent completion should not be marked delivered just because the internal completion user message reached the requester transcript. It must either be consumed by a following assistant response or fall back to the existing direct completion delivery path.

### Real environment tested

Local Linux worktree:

```text
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433
```

Branch / commit:

```text
feat/issue-92433 / b0783ee4ee
```

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run-state.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92433 && git diff --check
```

### Evidence after fix

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  6 passed (6)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.24s
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  100 passed (100)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.35s
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  117 passed (117)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.12s
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 45ms on 5 files using 1 threads.
```

```text
git diff --check exited successfully with no whitespace errors.
```

### Observed result after fix

The regression test now proves that a queued completion committed as a user `message_end` is rejected when requester `agent_end` arrives before assistant consumption. Completion handoff tests now expect `waitForAssistantResponseAfterTranscriptCommit: true` only on the active requester completion wake path, and the delivery/dispatch regression suite remains green.

### What was not tested

No live Discord, Telegram, Slack, Desktop, Mantis, or other channel transport proof was run in this environment. The issue was initialized under an explicit local/source-level proof boundary after live-proof and public-contract workflow blockers were bypassed for this task.

## Why it matters / User impact

This prevents a completed subagent result from disappearing when it lands at the exact boundary where the requester run is ending. Users should either see the completion through the active requester path or through the existing direct completion fallback instead of getting a silent drop.

## What did NOT change

Non-completion progress steering still resolves at the transcript commit boundary. The completion dispatch order remains direct-first before steer fallback. Channel transports, gateway send behavior, message-tool-only delivery, and timeout defaults were not changed.

## Architecture / source-of-truth check

The active requester transcript stream is used as the source of truth. A matching queued user `message_end` proves durability; a later assistant event proves requester consumption. Without the assistant event, terminal requester state is treated as unconfirmed completion delivery.

## Target test file

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.test.ts
src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts
```

## Scenario locked in

```text
queued completion user message_end -> requester agent_end -> no assistant response
```

The locked behavior is that this sequence rejects the active completion wake as unconsumed instead of reporting it as delivered.

## Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail

The new acknowledgement option is opt-in and enabled only for active requester completion handoff. It reuses existing queue outcome and direct fallback paths rather than adding a new delivery mechanism or broad channel-specific handling.

## Risk labels considered

```text
clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr
clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review
clawsweeper:needs-product-decision
nonlocal channel/live proof boundary
```

## Risk explanation

The patch touches runtime delivery acknowledgement semantics, so a maintainer should review the consumption boundary. The risk is contained because non-completion steering keeps its old behavior and unsupported transcript-wait targets still fall back to best-effort wake options.

## Why acceptable

The local proof deterministically exercises the reported interleaving at the runtime source level, and the patch turns the previously false delivered signal into the already-supported fallback flow without changing public channel contracts.
